_README: Per-row leg-state overlay for the v2 corpus (source of truth for legs; the manifest merges it in at build time). Same conventions as opg_legs_state.json: non-todo legs require commit+package provenance and a note; statement=done additionally requires the source-verification tuple on the manifest row (gated by check_milestone.py).

axv_1605_07411_00.note: Reconciled to chi_bounded.v:conj2_1605_statement. forall oriented H: chi_bounded_under (oriented G /\ ind_free H G) <-> oriented_forest H. Encodes Conj 2 'Forb(H) is chi-bounded iff H is a forest'. chi_bounded_under = exists f, chi(U(G)) <= f(omega(U(G))) [the paper's undirected chi/omega of the underlying graph, per docstring]; ind_free = no induced-subdigraph copy (arc-preserving-and-reflecting injection); oriented_forest = underlying graph of

axv_1605_07411_01.note: Reconciled to chi_bounded.v:conj4_1605_statement. forall oriented star S: chi_bounded_under (oriented G /\ ind_free S G). Encodes Conj 4 'for any oriented star S, Forb(S) is chi-bounded'. oriented_star = oriented + a centre c urel-adjacent to every other vertex with leaves pairwise non-adjacent = an orientation of K_{1,t}. Class = oriented graphs avoiding an induced S. Matches the row exactly. Faithful.

axv_1608_03040_00.note: Reconciled to colouring_variants.v:majority_3col_statement. `forall D:diGraphType, exists col:D->'I_3, majority_col col`, where majority_col = [forall v, 2*#|same_col_outnb col v| <= outdeg v] and same_col_outnb = out-neighbours w with col w = col v. This is exactly Conjecture 2 (Kreutzer-Oum-Seymour-van der Zypen-Wood, 1608.03040): 'Every digraph has a majority 3-colouring'. 2*#same<=outdeg encodes 'at most half of out-neighbours share the colour' faithfu

axv_1608_03040_01.note: Reconciled to colouring_variants.v:majority_k1col_statement. `forall k, 2<=k -> forall D, exists col:D->'I_k.+1, kmajority_col k col` with kmajority_col k = [forall v, k*#same <= outdeg v]. Matches Conjecture 9 (1608.03040): for k>=2 every digraph has a (k+1)-colouring with at most (1/k)deg+(v) same-coloured out-neighbours. k+1 colours (I_{k.+1}), k*#same<=outdeg <=> #same<=deg+/k, guard 2<=k all match. (Row status is 'disproved' mathematically; encoding is

axv_1608_03040_03.note: Reconciled to colouring_variants.v:majority_3col_tournament_statement. `forall T:tournament, exists col:T->'I_3, majority_col col`. Matches Open Problem 2 (1608.03040): 'Does every tournament have a majority 3-colouring?'. Object is the HB `tournament` structure (finite irreflexive complete digraph, core/tournament.v); majority_col reused. Faithful.

axv_1608_03040_04.note: Reconciled to colouring_variants.v:majority_3col_eulerian_statement. `forall D, eulerian D -> exists col:D->'I_3, majority_col col` with eulerian = [forall v, indeg v == outdeg v]. Matches Open Problem 3 (1608.03040): 'Does every Eulerian digraph have a majority 3-colouring?'. Caveat: `eulerian` encodes only the balanced (in=out at every vertex) condition, dropping the classical connectivity requirement; but majority colouring is a per-vertex local condition preser

axv_2009_13319_02.note: Reconciled to heroes_dichotomy.v:conj_4_4. forall (F:orientedDigraph)(l:nat), oriented_forest F -> dichromatic_bounded {D | oriented_dg D /\ no_induced_Kl l D /\ ind_free F D}. axv_2310_04265_03.note: Reconciled to twinwidth.v:conj_3_13_statement (re-encodings: conj_3_13_concrete). exists f, forall nonempty tournament T, exists order p:{perm T}, bclique p <= f(omegabar T) /\ otww_le p (f(omegabar T)). Encodes Conj 3.13 'exists f s.t. every T has an ordering with omega(T^p)<=f(omega-bar) AND tww(T,p)<=f(omega-bar)': bclique p = omega(T^p) backedge clique number, both conjuncts present with correct exists-f/forall-T/exists-p structure. axv_2310_04265_07.note: Reconciled to clique_cluster.v:dom_omega_cluster_statement. Rocq: exists f ell:nat->nat, forall (T:tournament)(k:nat), f k<=domnum T -> exists S:{set T}, #|S|<=ell k /\ k<=omegabar(sub_tournament S). Encodes AACL Conjecture 5.3 (Large dom implies an omegabar-cluster) = manifest #07, whose statement_text ('two functions f and ell ... every tournament T with dom(T)>=f(k) contains a subtournament X with |X|<=ell(k) and omegabar(X)>=k') matches verbatim. domnu

axv_2310_04265_09.note: Reconciled to clique_cluster.v:question_5_9_statement. Rocq: exists ell:nat->nat, forall (T:tournament)(k:nat), k<=omegabar(T) -> exists S:{set T}, #|S|<=ell k /\ k<=omegabar(sub_tournament S). Encodes AACL Question 5.9 (positive form): 'Is there a function ell such that, for every tournament T, if omegabar(T)>=k then T has a subtournament A with |A|<=ell(k) and omegabar(A)>=k.' Single function ell (f=id), object=tournament, sub_tournament S = induced

axv_2310_04265_10.note: Reconciled to clique_cluster.v:conjecture_5_10_statement. Rocq: forall k, 3<=k -> forall N, exists T:tournament, kcritical k T /\ N < #|T|. Encodes AACL (arXiv:2310.04265 v2) Conjecture 5.10 'For every integer k>=3, there are an infinite number of k-omegabar-critical tournaments.' kcritical (critical.v) = (omegabar T == k) && forall v, omegabar(del_tournament v)==k-1, exactly the paper's k-omegabar-critical definition. axv_2410_23566_00.note: Reconciled to unvd.v:prob_6 (re-encodings: prob6_unvd_statement). prob_6 := forall alpha:rat, exists a d b:nat, forall D with 0<#|D| and mad D <= alpha, for unvd-value nD: nD <= a*#|D|^d + b. drv_sad_wc3.note: Reconciled to sad.v:WC3_statement. forall D:diGraphType, (0<#|D|) -> arc_strong D 3 -> SAD D; arc_strong D 3 = every nonempty proper X has #|outcut X|>=3 (3-arc-strong), SAD D = arcset partitions into two disjoint covering spanning_strong parts. Matches drv_sad_wc3 'Every 3-arc-strong digraph D admits a strong arc decomposition: partition A(D)=A1|_|A2 with both spanning subdigraphs strongly connected'. Quantifier (forall D), object

drv_twoext_conjp.note: Reconciled to two_extremal.v:conjecture_P. forall D llD, two_extremal llD -> planar_sg(underlyingG llD). Matches drv_twoext_conjp exactly: 'for every 2-extremal digraph D, U(D) is planar.' two_extremal = k_extremal 2 = [strongb D, two_connected_sg(underlyingG), arc_conn=2, chi_vec_eq D 3] which is strong + U(D) 2-connected + lambda=2 + chi_vec=3 (chi_vec_eq D 3 = 0<3 /\ dicolorableb D 3 /\ ~~dicolorableb D 2 = chi_vec=3), matching the row'

drv_twoext_h6.note: Reconciled to two_extremal.v:H6_no_full_cover. forall D llD, (0<#|D|) -> strongb D -> Eulerian D -> arc_conn D = 2 -> three_connected_sg(underlyingG llD) -> ~connected[set:digonG llD] -> dicolorableb D 2. Encodes the criticality/no-full-cover barrier of drv_twoext_h6: 3-connected + Eulerian + lambda=2 + digon-free cut => chi_vec<=2.
